status: intermediate-values-locked; ciphertext requires reference impl run with a pinned 32-byte IV (MPC-Spec #9)
description: Byte-locks the deterministic intermediate values (wallet pub, BRC-42 invoice, shared_secret, HMAC offset) for the BRC-2 self-encryption derivation. The AES-256-GCM key is NOT the HMAC offset directly: per the canonical BSV SymmetricKey, the key is the x-coordinate of the derived child-key self-ECDH point. Full ciphertext byte-lock requires a reference impl run that injects the pinned IV (no SDK exposes IV injection in its production encrypt path; both impls add a test-only encrypt_with_iv seam for this vector).

iv_convention.rationale: 32-byte IV is the canonical BSV SymmetricKey convention across ALL ecosystem SDKs; a 12-byte IV matches none of them. Verified 2026-05-22: @bsv/sdk (TS) src/primitives/SymmetricKey.ts:44,86 (Random(32), ivLength=32); go-sdk primitives/ec/symmetric.go:29,64 (make([]byte,32), message[:32]); bsv-rs 0.3.11 (bsv-mpc) src/primitives/symmetric.rs:50; bsv-sdk 0.2.82 (rust-mpc) src/primitives/symmetric_key.rs:4 ('with the TS SDK's 32-byte IV convention'). NOTE: rust-mpc's 12-byte nonce in crates/storage/src/encryption.rs is its separate at-rest storage layer, NOT the BRC-2 presig path. Both presig paths delegate to their SDK's 32-byte SymmetricKey. The prior 12-byte aes_gcm_iv_hex values in this vector were mis-authored placeholders and have been corrected to 32 bytes.
iv_convention.corrected: 2026-05-22 (Calhoun; both stewards have authority per partnership playbook). The expected ciphertexts remain TBD pending the reference run under MPC-Spec #9 using these corrected 32-byte IVs.

negative_tests.name: wrong-presig_id-fails-decrypt
negative_tests.description: Encrypt with presig_id='presig-aaa'; decrypt with 'presig-bbb' MUST fail.

negative_tests.name: wrong-wallet-fails-decrypt
negative_tests.description: Encrypt with wallet A; decrypt with wallet B MUST fail.

negative_tests.name: tampered-ciphertext-fails-decrypt
negative_tests.description: Encrypt normally; flip last byte; decrypt MUST fail (AES-GCM tag).

negative_tests.name: different-presig_id-different-ciphertext
negative_tests.description: SAME plaintext, two different presig_ids → ciphertexts MUST differ.
